Remark 1.4.2.

In Theorem 1.4.1, the assumptions are only sufficient, but not necessary conditions. E.g. \(f_n(x)= x^n/n\) converges to \(f(x)=0\) uniformly on \(I=(-1,1)\text{,}\) and \(f(x)=0\) is differentiable on \(I\text{,}\) yet the convergence of \(f'_n(x)=x^{n-1}\) (to \(f'(x)=0\)) is not uniform on \(I\) (although when restricted to \((-\delta, \delta)\) for any fixed \(0 < \delta < 1\text{,}\) the convergence is uniform.). Another example is \(f_n(x)= \sin (nx)/n\text{,}\) which converges to \(f(x)=0\) uniformly on \(\mathbb R\text{,}\) yet \(f'_n(x)= \cos (nx)\) would not converge for many values of \(x\) (e.g. all rational multiples of \(\pi\)).
in-context
